Entity_Description: Aldergrove is a semi-rural community in the Township of Langley within Metro Vancouver, British Columbia, straddling the boundary with the City of Abbotsford in the eastern Fraser Valley. Home to roughly 12,000 residents, it sits about 53 kilometres southeast of Vancouver. Aldergrove is known for the Greater Vancouver Zoo, Aldergrove Regional Park with its swimming lake, and the Aldergrove-Lynden border crossing into Washington State via Highway 13. Surrounding Agricultural Land Reserve farmland supports blueberry, berry and dairy operations alongside newer residential subdivisions.

Short_Description: Straddling the Langley-Abbotsford line at the end of Highway 13, Aldergrove is the Fraser Valley community where Metro Vancouver's suburbs give way to blueberry fields, dairy acreages and the Aldergrove-Lynden border crossing into Washington. It is also home to the Greater Vancouver Zoo and the swimming lake at Aldergrove Regional Park. Properties here range from long-established farm parcels and character homes along Fraser Highway to fast-growing subdivisions on the community's south side. {COMPANY_NAME} delivers {SERVICE} tailored to that mix, giving every {PROPERTY_TYPE} owner {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} from a {BUSINESS_TYPE} who genuinely knows the area. For {ESTIMATE_TYPE}, call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Aldergrove sits at the meeting point of two worlds: it is technically part of the Township of Langley in Metro Vancouver, yet its eastern edge along County Line Road spills into the City of Abbotsford, and much of what surrounds it is working Agricultural Land Reserve farmland. Drive south on Highway 13 (264th Street) and the road ends at the quiet Aldergrove-Lynden crossing into Washington State; head north along Fraser Highway and you pass the Greater Vancouver Zoo, one of the region's best-known attractions. This is a place defined by contrasts, and any {BUSINESS_TYPE} serving it well has to understand every one of them.

The housing stock reflects that split personality. Older character homes and long-held farm parcels sit on generous acreages along the rural concessions, while newer master-planned subdivisions have filled in the community's south and west sides with tightly spaced single-family homes and townhomes. That range means {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} looks very different from one street to the next, and {COMPANY_NAME} adjusts its approach accordingly rather than treating every {PROPERTY_TYPE} the same.

Climate shapes the work here as much as anywhere in the Lower Mainland. Aldergrove's mild oceanic weather brings heavy rainfall from November through March, and that persistent moisture drives drainage headaches, moss and algae growth, and exterior wear on both barn-adjacent outbuildings and newer stucco-and-vinyl subdivision homes. The warm, dry summers that follow create their own seasonal demands. Our team plans {SERVICE} around this rhythm so problems are addressed before the wet season sets in.

Agriculture remains the economic backbone of the surrounding land - blueberry fields, berry farms and dairy operations stretch out toward Bradner to the west and the Abbotsford line to the east - while many residents commute to Langley City, Abbotsford or into Metro Vancouver for work. That mix of farm properties, rural residences and commuter subdivisions is exactly why local knowledge matters. Whether a job is near Aldergrove Regional Park and its popular swimming lake, out on the acreages by the zoo, or in a new development off 272nd Street, {COMPANY_NAME} brings {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} and a reputation built {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} in the making. {TAGLINE} Reach us anytime to put a trusted {SERVICE_CATEGORY} specialist to work on your property.
Local_Facts:
- Aldergrove is a community in the Township of Langley, Metro Vancouver, straddling the boundary with the City of Abbotsford.
- The community has approximately 12,000 residents (GeoNames estimate).
- Aldergrove is home to the Greater Vancouver Zoo, a long-established regional attraction.
- Aldergrove Regional Park features a popular swimming lake within the Aldergrove Bowl.
- The Aldergrove-Lynden border crossing connects the community to Washington State via Highway 13 (264th Street).
- Downtown Vancouver lies about 53 kilometres to the northwest.
- The surrounding Agricultural Land Reserve supports blueberry, berry and dairy farming.
